AFRL’s Munitions Directorate develops advanced conventional weapons technologies to deliver more effective, affordable, and precise munitions for multi-domain operations.
AFRL’s Sensors Directorate develops sensor and countermeasure technologies for reconnaissance, surveillance, precision engagement, and electronic warfare to secure operational advantage.
AFRL’s Space Vehicles Directorate develops and transitions space technologies that strengthen communications, missile warning, navigation, and broader space mission capabilities for the warfighter.

NAVAIR's mission is to provide full life-cycle support of naval aviation aircraft, weapons and systems operated by Sailors and Marines. This support includes research, design, development and systems engineering, acquisition, test and evaluation, training facilities and equipment, repair and modification, and in-service engineering and logistics support.
NAVSEA's primary objective is to engineer, build, buy, and maintain the U.S. Navy's fleet of ships and its combat systems.

NAWCTSD delivers research, development, acquisition, and lifecycle support for advanced training systems that prepare warfighters through simulation and mission-focused learning technologies.
NAWCWD at China Lake delivers full-spectrum weapons research, development, test, and evaluation through its land ranges and technical expertise to advance naval and joint warfighting capabilities.
NAWCWD at Point Mugu delivers maritime and airborne test, evaluation, electronic warfare, and range capabilities that strengthen naval weapons development and operational effectiveness.

The DEVCOM Analysis Center (DAC) and The Research Analysis Center (TRAC) have transitioned together to a new organization called the Transformation Decision Analysis Center (TDAC) in February 2026. The Defense Microelectronics Activity delivers microelectronics products, services, and technical expertise that sustain weapon systems readiness and strengthen the defense industrial base.
